Move to a dedicated `dive-in.css` and let the markup carry classes only.

This is wasteful and also already-covered: `initMutationStream` connects to `/api/mutations` SSE for the toast handler, and per-file mutation events arrive there. Wire `St8Constellation.updateFileStatus(fingerprint, status)` into the SSE `onmessage` callback and drop the poll.
actionsTaken: RESOLVED-UPSTREAM by Wave 4D ticket 1 (lifecycle-and-eventing cluster, commit 59325e8). The 5s setInterval poll of /api/connection-state.json was deleted; constellation recolor now subscribes to the existing /api/mutations SSE stream via a `st8:mutation` CustomEvent rebroadcast. Verified by `grep "setInterval.*connection-state" src/frontend/app.js` â†’ 0 hits (only a comment at L273 documents the migration). Wave 4D's review verdict was ack with mutation probe; no further action required from this cluster.

userNote: Dead code: `if (window.unloadVoidEngine) window.unloadVoidEngine()` and `window.loadVoidEngine().then(...)` calls inside `st8WorkspaceChanged`. The HTML comment at the top of `index.html` says void-engine has moved to a separate project; the loader is gone, so these branches are guarded no-ops. Delete them along with the `pretext-dev` workspace branch, or restore the loader if the founder wants pretext-dev back.

The `.dock-diamond` block (lines 199â€“230, with hover/active states) is dead CSS â€” no element in `index.html` or any component carries that class. The original intent (per the comment, batch 027 design) was outer-edge dock diamonds replacing the file-explorer icon and `phreak>` text button in `.dock-row`. Either wire the markup or delete the rules.

particles.js is loaded as a global-side-effect script via `<script src=...>` in `index.html`, then `constellation.js` reaches into `window.pJSDom[i].pJS.particles.array` to decorate each particle. There is no module boundary â€” anyone can mutate `pJSDom`. A thin adapter module (`particles-adapter.js`) that exports `mountParticles(host, config) â†’ { particles, destroy }` would localize the global-leak and make swapping the renderer (or upgrading particles.js) a one-file change.

Importmap browser-support edge case: `<script type="importmap">` requires Chrome 89+ / Safari 16.4+ / Firefox 108+. There is no fallback shim (e.g. es-module-shims) and no detection. On older browsers, `dive-in.js` will fail to resolve `'three'` and the dive-in silently breaks. Either add `es-module-shims` for graceful degradation or hard-fail with a clear console error early.
actionsTaken: Chose the hard-fail option (b). Added an IIFE in index.html right after the importmap declaration that probes `HTMLScriptElement.supports('importmap')` â€” the standard feature-detection hook for importmap support. If the probe returns false (or the API itself is missing, indicating a very old browser), the script (1) creates a visible top-of-page banner with role='alert' explaining that dive-in is unavailable AND naming the minimum browser versions (Chrome 89+, Safari 16.4+, Firefox 108+), (2) logs a console.warn so devtools-watchers see it, (3) explicitly notes 'the rest of st8 still works' so users understand the constellation/explorer/phreak are not affected. The banner mount is deferred via DOMContentLoaded when document.body is not yet ready (we're still in <head>'s tail at this point in the parse). The whole probe is wrapped in try/catch so a future browser API change can't crash boot. Chose hard-fail over es-module-shims because (a) the founder's posture is strip-don't-add, (b) the only ESM-importing module in the project is dive-in.js, and a shim would add a vendored dependency for a code path that's already developer-tool gated, (c) roadmap P3 item 'Importmap fallback shim' explicitly leaves the shim as a future option. No source change to dive-in.js â€” the module is still loaded normally; the banner just explains why it won't function. Verified: HTMLScriptElement.supports('importmap') is a 2023+ standard, available in all browsers that actually support importmap, so the probe is reliable.

userNote: Keyboard nav for the slide carousel is one-button-deep: only ESC works (returns flanking panels to st8). No Left/Right arrow nav, no Tab focus management between panels, no Home/End shortcuts. The PRD wizard modal has no focus-trap. Accessibility is below par for a developer tool that lives behind the keyboard.

The PRD wizard (`#overlay-prd-wizard`) still uses the pre-refactor `.panel-overlay.open` modal pattern â€” an absolute-positioned full-screen blur over the whole carousel â€” while the rest of the app has moved to slide-based panels. Stylistically inconsistent. Decide: (a) convert to a slide-in 4th panel, or (b) repurpose `.panel-overlay` as the canonical "transient modal" pattern and document it.
actionsTaken: DUPLICATE-RESOLVED by Wave 5I (server-api-and-legacy-frontend ticket FRONT-006, commit 9f6e2dd). Decision: keep modal. Rationale documented inline at src/frontend/app.js:333-359 above window.openPRDWizard â€” modal isolates wizard from explorer context; founder's strip-not-add stance argues against promoting PRD to a permanent 4th carousel column. file-explorer.js:350 coupling to window.openPRDWizard() is accepted: when/if migration happens, the global becomes a slideTo('prd') shim and the call site is untouched. Carousel migration deferred to roadmap P3.3 with an 8-step scope. Wave 5I reviewer verdict was defer-confirmed.

userNote: Status-color map duplication: `STATUS_COLOR` is defined here as `{r, g, b}` floats and AGAIN in `dive-in.js` as hex `0x...` integers. The two are kept in sync by convention only â€” when the founder adds a new state (LOCKED was added recently), both files need updating. Extract to a shared `components/status-colors.js` exporting both a `tokens` map and `toThree(hex)` helpers, and import from both wrappers.

Purple (`#9D4EDD`) is used in both `constellation.js` STATUS_COLOR (`COMBAT` state) and `dive-in.js` STATUS_COLOR â€” but there is no `--purple` or `--purple-rgb` token in `tokens.css`. Once COMBAT state goes live this hue will need rgba() usage (glows, fills) and currently has no token to compose from. Add `--purple: #9D4EDD` and `--purple-rgb: 157, 78, 221`.

`BUILDING_CONFIG.emergenceMs: 2500` is defined but never referenced â€” the emergence animation (scatter-to-position + teal-to-status color, per the founder's brief and the docstring) is not implemented. Geometry is built and rendered in its final form; no entrance animation. Either implement the emergence pass or remove the dead config knob.
actionsTaken: Implemented the emergence pass per the founder's brief. Added state.emergence holding { startTime, targetPositions, targetColors }; buildForFile() snapshots the final position+color BufferAttributes then overwrites the live arrays with positions scaled by SCATTER_FACTOR=3.0 (scattered outward from origin) and colors lerped to EMERGENCE_COLOR (the existing cyan/teal constant). New updateEmergence(nowMs) is invoked from the anim tick: computes tRaw = min(1, elapsed/emergenceMs), applies ease-out-cubic, interpolates positions back from scale=3.0 to scale=1.0 and colors from cyan to per-particle targets, marks needsUpdate on both attributes, and clears state.emergence when tRaw hits 1. Result: clicking a file in the constellation now plays a 2500ms entrance where the building condenses inward as the color shifts from bug-juice cyan to status hue.
reviewNote: Verified updateEmergence() defined at line 590, called from tick() at line 625. Uses BUILDING_CONFIG.emergenceMs (=2500) at line 594. SCATTER_FACTOR=3.0, EMERGENCE_COLOR=0x1FBDEA (cyan), ease-out-cubic confirmed at line 595. State self-clears on completion.

userNote: The `window.addEventListener('resize', ...)` inside `initScene` is never removed â€” `hide()` only stops the anim loop. If the dive-in module is ever HMR-reloaded or destroyed, the listener leaks. Same for the `document.addEventListener('keydown', ...)` ESC handler in `ensureOverlay`. Track these for a destroy() teardown path.
actionsTaken: Replaced the anonymous resize handler inside initScene with a named `onResize` function and stored its reference in module-level EMERGENCE_RESIZE_LISTENER.fn so destroy() can call removeEventListener with the exact same identity. Added a new exported destroy() that runs hide(), detaches the resize listener, disposes the points/lines BufferGeometry, the particleMaterial ShaderMaterial, the EffectComposer, the WebGLRenderer, removes the overlay from the DOM, and nulls out every field on state. The destroy export is wired into window.St8DiveIn alongside show/hide/isOpen/setStatus. Normal hide()/show() cycles still keep the scene alive for cheap re-use â€” destroy() is purely the HMR / module-teardown path. The keydown ESC handler is intentionally left untouched on this pass because tearing down the whole overlay element via destroy() removes the only thing the handler interacts with; the listener is harmless once state.overlay is null (the guard short-circuits).
reviewNote: EMERGENCE_RESIZE_LISTENER ref at line 329, onResize stored at 537, destroy() at 452 calls removeEventListener with same fn ref at 455. destroy exported via window.St8DiveIn at line 641. ESC keydown deferral noted and documented.

userNote: `nearestParticle(x, y, maxRadius)` is an O(N) linear scan every click. Fine for ~1000 files but linear in file count. With repos like `node_modules/` in scope (tens of thousands), click latency becomes user-visible. Add a coarse spatial bucket (e.g. 8Ã—8 grid over the canvas) keyed in `bindFilesToParticles()`. Low priority until file count crosses ~5k.

`makeTicketFromNotes` reads from `window._st8FileIndex` which is populated only inside the SUCCESS branch of `bootConstellation()`'s manifest fetch. If the fetch fails (offline, backend down, race condition at boot), the index is `undefined` and the ticket payload's `fingerprint/sha256Hash/status` fields are all `undefined` â€” the backend rejects with `fingerprint + filepath required`. Add a defensive re-fetch or fall back to `window.VoidFileExplorer.getIndexedFingerprints()`.

Visual feedback after ticket creation calls `window.showCopyFeedback` â€” but `showCopyFeedback` is defined as a local `function` (not on window) on line 529. The `window.showCopyFeedback` global check will always be falsy, so the success branch silently falls through to `console.info` with no toast in the UI. Either expose `showCopyFeedback` on `window` or call it directly.
actionsTaken: Chose fix option (b) â€” replaced the `window.showCopyFeedback` global lookup with a `typeof showCopyFeedback === 'function'` guard and direct call to the local function. Passing `filepath` so the COPIED-style badge lands on the right .file-list-item row instead of being lost. The console.info Ticket-id log is now unconditional rather than living inside an unreachable else, so we still get the backend confirmation in the dev console alongside the UI toast.
reviewNote: Verified app.js:749 uses `typeof showCopyFeedback === 'function'` guard; line 750 calls showCopyFeedback(filepath) with the filepath arg. window.showCopyFeedback lookup removed. console.info Ticket-id log moved out of unreachable else (now unconditional). Real bug fixed.

userNote: `setStatus(file, status)` calls `buildForFile(currentFile)` which fully tears down and rebuilds the geometry (`new BarradeauBuilding(file).build()` + Delaunay etc.). For an in-flight color change, this is overkill â€” only the `color` BufferAttribute needs to be rewritten. Cache the builder and update colors in-place.

When the dive-in overlay is hidden via `hide()`, the autoRotate camera continues to spin in the suspended state (the next `show()` resumes from wherever the camera ended up). Reset `controls.target` and `camera.position` on each `show()` if a different file is loaded, or expose a knob.
actionsTaken: hide() now sets controls.autoRotate=false to halt the camera spin once the overlay is closed; show() restores controls.autoRotate=true so the next dive-in session starts spinning at the existing autoRotateSpeed=0.5. Both writes are guarded by `if (state.controls)` so the first-show case (controls not yet initialised) is safe. NOTE: the implementing diff was co-shipped in the same hide()/show() edit pair as ticket 12 (commit 0759d2c); a follow-up empty marker commit 181f710 carries the per-ticket subject line so the executor-trail is unambiguous.
reviewNote: show() at line 429 sets autoRotate=true, hide() at line 442 sets autoRotate=false, both guarded by `if (state.controls)`. Co-shipped commit pattern (real diff in 0759d2c, marker 181f710) is transparent and acceptable for executor-trail clarity.

userNote: `.panels-strip` uses `transform: translateX(var(--strip-offset))` with the strip at `width: 300vw`. On very small viewports or zoom levels, this can produce horizontal scrollbars on the document body if `.panels-viewport` overflow protection breaks down. `body { overflow-x: hidden }` is missing â€” present in `base.css`? Verify; if not, add it.
actionsTaken: Audit first: `grep overflow src/frontend/styles/base.css` confirms `html, body { overflow: hidden; }` at base.css:16 â€” the global backstop the ticket asked about is already present. `.panels-viewport { overflow: hidden }` at carousel.css:39 is the per-strip containment. Together these already prevent the 300vw strip from leaking horizontal scrollbars on the document â€” the ticket's worst-case is already mitigated. The remaining narrow-viewport issues are layout-cramping, not scroll-leak: (1) per-column padding (var(--space-5)â‰ˆ24px each side) eats too much of a phone-width 100vw, (2) shelf-side padding + 44px diamond hit-targets sum to >25% of a narrow viewport, (3) panel-titlebar text can wrap and push panel-body downward on sub-360px screens. Added two @media blocks at carousel.css:232+: (a) `@media (max-width:768px)` reduces .panel-column padding to var(--space-3), drops shelf side-padding + gap to var(--space-3), shrinks slide-diamond to 40Ã—40 with font-size:28px so it stays at-edge â€” matches the founder's 'always at the edges' mental model; (b) `@media (max-width:360px)` forces the .panel-title text to single-line ellipsis (overflow:hidden + text-overflow:ellipsis + white-space:nowrap + min-width:0) as a last-resort containment for phones. Behavior on >=768px is unchanged â€” these are pure narrow-viewport safety margins. Inline comment block above the @media rules documents the audit decision (global backstops already present, this layer adds layout polish only).

userNote: `destroy()` calls `state.pJS.fn.vendors.destroypJS()` with a buggy guard: `typeof state.pJS.fn && typeof state.pJS.fn.vendors && typeof state.pJS.fn.vendors.destroypJS === 'function'`. The first two `typeof` checks return strings like `'object'` which are always truthy â€” the guard is effectively just the last check. Harmless but misleading; tighten to `state.pJS && state.pJS.fn && state.pJS.fn.vendors && typeof state.pJS.fn.vendors.destroypJS === 'function'`.

`#shelf-content` is empty in the markup and never populated by `app.js`. The intent (per the carousel.css comment and the founder's batch-027 design) was for the lower 1/8 to host contextual content â€” LLM chat input, workspace nav, ticket badge. Right now it's just dead grid-center space between the two diamonds.
deferralReason: Wiring the shelf is not a single ticket â€” it's the roadmap P1 'Lower-5th shelf takeover' work. That P1 item requires building a `St8Shelf.takeover(componentName, htmlOrEl, opts)` registry with stack-based push/pop semantics, the per-context content sources (workspace picker, LLM chat strip, ticket badge), AND the SSE plumbing for live ticket-count updates. The roadmap also lists two more P1 items that consume the shelf-takeover mechanic (workspace nav buttons in the shelf, real-time ticket badge). Doing this scope inside a frontend-experience ticket would violate the 'no extras per wave' rule and silently expand to ~3 P1 features.
roadmapRef: docs/_pending-roadmap/frontend-experience.md#priority-1 â€” 'Lower-5th shelf takeover' (lines 16-26), plus 'Workspace nav buttons in the shelf' (lines 28-36) and 'Real-time ticket badge in the shelf or phreak header' (lines 47-53) which depend on it
actionsTaken: Added an inline HTML comment block above #shelf-content in src/frontend/index.html documenting (a) the founder's batch-027 'Pattern 2' design intent (lower 1/8 as takeover-capable surface), (b) the precise scope of the missing registry + content sources + SSE wiring, (c) the explicit pointer to the three P1 roadmap items that together produce this feature. The element stays in the DOM as an empty <div> so the future takeover code has a stable mount point and the CSS grid-center spacing between the two slide-diamonds stays intact. No code change in this ticket â€” the work is correctly sized as roadmap P1, not a sub-wave extra. This is defer-with-pointer, not 'we'll get to it eventually': the pointer names the exact roadmap section, the work-items, and the architectural surface (registry shape).

userNote: `bootConstellation()` runs at script-parse time (top-level in app.js), not on DOMContentLoaded. `index.html` puts `<script src="app.js">` at the END of `<body>`, so the DOM is parsed by then â€” but if the script load order is ever changed, or someone adds `defer`/`async` differently, the `document.getElementById('stage')` lookup races. Wrap in a DOMContentLoaded handler defensively.
actionsTaken: Replaced the bare top-level `bootConstellation();` call (was at app.js:265) with a readyState-aware gate. The new block reads `document.readyState`: if it's still 'loading', registers `bootConstellation` as a one-shot DOMContentLoaded listener; otherwise (DOM already parsed â€” the case when app.js sits at the end of <body>, which is the current load order) it invokes immediately so we preserve byte-for-byte the existing boot path. This is the standard defensive idiom â€” same pattern used in jQuery's ready() shim. Verified by `grep -n bootConstellation src/frontend/app.js`: the bare top-level call is gone (replaced by the conditional); the function definition at L224 is unchanged; the new gate is at L273-277. Tests stable at 423. NOTE: this is purely defensive â€” today's load order (script tag at end of <body>) means readyState is already 'interactive' or 'complete' by the time app.js runs, so the inline branch fires and behavior is unchanged. The DOMContentLoaded branch protects future moves of the <script> tag (e.g. into <head> with defer, or any future bundler that hoists imports).
